mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate over the trailing 3 months lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically raising present value and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of regime.
failure_condition: Rank IC between 3-month FEDFUNDS change and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months, it signals a labor market inflection that precedes earnings downgrades and risk-off equity flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward 20d returns following triggered UNRATE rises are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 across walk-forward folds.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in HOUST and RSAFS growth signals broad-based consumer and housing demand weakness that compounds into a sharper equity drawdown than either series alone would predict, producing negative S&P 500 returns over a full quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3mo growth < 0 AND RSAFS 3mo growth < 0 simultaneously
failure_condition: Joint HOUST/RSAFS decline instances produce positive 60d forward returns in >50% of historical occurrences, or the interaction term adds no incremental Rank IC over univariate models (delta IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening regime where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4%, further rate increases raise borrowing costs enough to compress near-term equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week; in low-rate regimes the same rate move has negligible effect.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive rate level
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS increases above the 4% threshold are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10) across walk-forward folds, or direction flips positive in majority of high-rate tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in INDPRO reflects improving industrial output and forward earnings momentum, which equity markets price in quickly, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days on an always-on basis.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO MoM change and forward 5d returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS month-over-month change falls below zero, signaling outright payroll contraction, it triggers a recession-risk repricing in equities that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month, whereas positive payroll growth carries no strong directional signal.
failure_condition: 20d forward returns following negative PAYEMS prints are positive in more than 55% of historical triggered instances, or Rank IC falls below 0.03 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of decelerating GDPC1 growth alongside elevated CPIAUCSL inflation (a stagflationary signature) compounds margin compression and growth risk premia beyond what either series signals alone, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth < 1.5% AND CPIAUCSL YoY > 4%
failure_condition: Joint low-growth/high-inflation episodes show non-negative 60d forward returns in majority of historical cases, or interaction model fails to beat univariate GDPC1-only model by Rank IC margin of 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation exceeding 4%, positive inflation surprises are interpreted as increasing the odds of continued monetary tightening, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month; in low-inflation regimes the same signal has no effect.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY > 4% and FEDFUNDS in tightening trend
failure_condition: 20d forward returns following high-inflation prints under tightening conditions are not significantly negative (p > 0.10), or effect reverses sign when tested against low-inflation subsample baseline.
